====Pokémon====
This listing is of Lt. Surge's {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in the [[Pokémon Adventures]] manga.

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Lt. Surge
|pkmn=Electabuzz
|gender=male
|type1=Electric
|img=Lt Surge Electabuzz Adventures.png
|epnum=PS011
|epname=Buzz Off, Electabuzz!
|desc={{p|Electabuzz}} is one of Lt. Surge's most powerful Pokémon and he noted that he is disobedient and Surge couldn't even get it in a [[Poké Ball]]. He was first seen on the [[S.S. Anne]], where Surge sent him out to fight against {{adv|Red}} and his [[Poli|Poliwhirl]]. He seemingly had the upper hand until the {{type|Water}} Pokémon evolved into a {{p|Poliwrath}} after being pushed off the side of the ship, which resulted in Red defeating Surge and Electabuzz. He was used to fight against Red again during the battle at [[Silph Co.]]. Two years later, Electabuzz was used in the battle of [[Cerise Island]], where he helped battle [[Bruno]], but was not able to damage his Pokémon due to their evasiveness. During the Gym Leader Tournament in [[Johto]], he fought against [[Morty]]'s {{p|Gastly}} and {{p|Misdreavus}}, who almost defeated him before Surge switched him with {{p|Magnemite}}.

Electabuzz's known moves are {{m|Thunder Punch}}, {{m|Thunderbolt}}, {{m|Thunder Shock}}, and {{m|Thunder}}, and his Ability is {{a|Static}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Lt. Surge
|pkmn=Magneton (multiple)
|type1=Electric
|type2=Steel
|img=Lt Surge Magneton Adventures.png
|cap=Lt. Surge's Magneton
|epnum=PS011
|epname=Buzz Off, Electabuzz!
|desc=Lt. Surge owns many {{p|Magneton}} and uses them for aerial transportation by having them create an energy platform using their magnetic charge. He has carried (and sometimes trapped) many people using this ability, from allies like {{adv|Bill}} and {{Adv|Gold}} and enemies such as {{adv|Red}}.

Magneton's known moves are {{m|Supersonic}}, {{m|Sonic Boom}}, {{m|Flash}}, and {{m|Thunderbolt}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Lt. Surge
|pkmn=Magnemite (multiple)
|type1=Electric
|type2=Steel
|img=Lt Surge Magnemite Adventures.png
|cap=Lt. Surge's Magnemite
|epnum=PS010
|epname=Danger: High Voltorb
|desc=Lt. Surge owns many {{p|Magnemite}} that first appeared acting somewhat like guards of the S.S. Anne. In Johto, Surge used one in the Gym Leader Tournament, where it defeated Morty's Misdreavus before it fainted from the effect of {{m|Destiny Bond}}, resulting in a tie between the two Gym Leaders.

Magnemite's known moves are {{m|Protect}} and {{m|Zap Cannon}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Lt. Surge
|pkmn=Voltorb and Electrode (multiple)
|type1=Electric
|img=Lt Surge Voltorb Electrode Adventures.png
|cap=Lt. Surge's Voltorb and Electrode
|epnum=PS010
|epname=Danger: High Voltorb
|desc=The main bulk of Lt. Surge's force resides in the power of his {{p|Voltorb}} and {{p|Electrode}}'s {{m|Selfdestruct}}s, and he has many of them on his team. This strategy was particularly instrumental in the defeat of Bruno when one of Surge's Electrodes used its Selfdestruct on {{adv|Bill}}'s {{p|Exeggcute}}'s {{m|Egg Bomb}}, resulting in an explosion that took down [[Bruno's Onix]], which was being used as a platform for the battle. They also later proved handy when their combined Selfdestruct attacks aided in his narrow escape from the [[Masked Man]].

Voltorb's only known move is {{m|Thunder Wave}}.

Electrode's only known move is {{m|Self-Destruct}}.}}

{{anchor|Raichu 2}}{{TrainerPoké
|main=yes
|trainer=Lt. Surge
|gender=male
|pkmn=Raichu
|type1=Electric
|img=Lt Surge Raichu Adventures.png
|epnum=PS029
|epname=Go for the Golbat
|desc=Lt. Surge's {{p|Raichu}} was very briefly seen at Silph Co. but didn't participate in battle until Surge went to Johto. There, he was used to battle the [[Masked Man]]'s {{p|Houndour}} and to very briefly fight against the [[Red Gyarados]] before he realized that he wasn't the enemy, as he was trying to free {{adv|Silver}}.}}

=====Released=====
{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Lt. Surge
|pkmn=Zapdos
|type1=Electric
|type2=Flying
|img=Lt Surge Zapdos Adventures.png
|epnum=PS029
|epname=Go for the Golbat
|main=Legendary birds (Adventures)
|main_section=Zapdos
|desc={{p|Zapdos}} was used as an experiment from Team Rocket. He took control of it prior to ''[[PS030|Zap! Zap! Zapdos]]''. Around the end of the battle against Red, it ended up shocking Lt. Surge with its tremendous {{m|Thunder}} attack after [[Saur]]'s {{m|Razor Leaf}} tore up his insulating underwear, rendering it useless and nearly killing him. As a result, Red earned his Badge and stole his electric-proof gloves. While he was knocked out cold, Zapdos was called upon by [[Sabrina's Kadabra]] and was merged into {{DL|Legendary birds (Adventures)|Thu-Fi-Zer}}, the Rockets' ultimate creation. After its defeat, it flew away alongside its fellow [[legendary birds]] and was later recaught by {{adv|Green}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Lt. Surge
|pkmn=Raikou
|type1=Electric
|img=Lt Surge Raikou Adventures.png
|epnum=PS117
|epname=Slugging it out with Slugma
|main=Legendary beasts (Adventures)
|main_section=Raikou
|desc={{p|Raikou}} was partnered up with Lt. Surge in the battle against the [[Masked Man]]. After Surge was rendered inoperative by the villain, it partnered up with {{adv|Gold}} instead, and went back to the wild when everything had been resolved. Much later, it was recaught by [[Anabel]] in [[Johto]] and became her major powerhouse.}}

=====Temporary=====
{{TrainerPoké
|main=Silver's Gyarados
|trainer=Silver
|pkmn=Gyarados
|shiny=yes
|gender=male
|type1=Water
|type2=Flying
|img=Lt Surge Gyarados Adventures.png
|epnum=PS112
|epname=Raise the Red Gyarados
|desc=Lt. Surge found this special {{p|Gyarados}} after being frozen in the Lake of Rage in ''[[PS140|Do-Si-Do with Dodrio]]''. This happened during Gold and Silver's battle with the Masked Man. He returned him to Silver afterwards.}}
